summary: Complete signal source map for confluence engine forensic analysis

confluence.description: Signal + sweep fire same direction → +0.05 HC boost

ucl_confirmed.description: Wide-stop sweep rescued via UCL confirmation

architecture.description:
The confluence engine uses a HYBRID architecture:

1. AGGREGATOR (MULTIPLICATIVE context): OB, FVG, sweep, VWAP, delta, trend
   signals are combined INTERNALLY into a single weighted score. They are NOT
   independent trigger sources — they must collectively reach >= 0.75 to fire.
   Individual signal types CANNOT independently generate trades through the
   aggregator path. Weight distribution: Technical 50%, Discord 25%, ML 25%.
   Minimum 2 aligned signals required.

2. SWEEP DETECTOR (ADDITIVE trigger): The LiquiditySweepDetector runs in
   PARALLEL to the aggregator and CAN independently generate trades when
   sweep score >= 0.70 and passes HC filter >= 0.75. This is the ONLY
   signal source that operates as an additive trigger.

3. Three entry modes:
   - signal-only: aggregator fires, no sweep → entry_source='signal'
   - sweep-only: sweep fires, no aggregator signal → entry_source='sweep'
   - confluence: both fire same direction → +0.05 HC boost, entry_source='confluence'

Knowledge base design (Section 19: HTF 30%, PA 25%, Vol 15%, ML 30%) is
NOT what was implemented. Actual weights: Technical 50%, Discord 25%, ML 25%.
Discord signals are deprecated (HTF Bias Engine replaced them).
ML predictor is None (placeholder). So effective weight is 100% technical.

CRITICAL: The 'FVG', 'order_block', 'structure_shift', 'session',
'displacement' signal types referenced in the task do NOT exist as
independent trigger sources. They are INTERNAL to the aggregator's
_extract_technical_signals() method. The actual individual technical
signals are: bullish/bearish_order_block, bullish/bearish_fvg,
buy/sell_side_sweep, vwap_above/below, delta_divergence, trend_up/down.
These combine into the aggregated 'signal' entry source.

institutional_modifiers: gamma_regime (position multiplier from VIX term structure)
